raw_note_text:
PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Chronic DVT of the left leg (on Eliquis), Hypertension, GERD. 
ASSESSMENT: Acute cellulitis of the left lower extremity. No evidence of acute DVT on Doppler ultrasound performed today. Plan: IV Vancomycin.
explanation: Active diagnosis is cellulitis. DVT is historical/chronic and Doppler was negative for acute process. System must not activate I82.40x.

raw_note_text:
65yo male with chest pain. 
Differential diagnosis includes: 
1. Rule out PE (negative CTA)
2. Rule out MI (negative Troponins)
3. Rule out Dissection (negative CTA)
4. Community acquired pneumonia - Right lower lobe infiltrate seen on CXR. 
STARTING AZITHROMYCIN.
explanation: PE, MI, and Dissection were ruled out. Pneumonia is the only active confirmed diagnosis.

raw_note_text:
Postoperative Note: 
Procedure: Left total hip arthroplasty (THA). 
Indications: Severe osteoarthritis of the right hip. 
Findings: Advanced chondromalacia of the left acetabulum. 
Postop Diagnosis: Left hip osteoarthritis.
explanation: Conflicting laterality in note. Procedural evidence and final diagnosis point to LEFT (M16.12). RIGHT mention in indications is likely a template error.
